response.overview: A Life in Pardah is a documentary exploring the lived realities of women in parts of Pakistan and South Asia where social expectations, cultural traditions, and informal systems of control shape everyday life. The film looks at how “pardah” operates not only as a physical practice, but also as a broader social condition that influences mobility, education, expression, and personal autonomy.Through observational storytelling and personal accounts, the documentary highlights how these structures are experienced in different ways across generations and social settings. Rather than focusing on a single narrative, it brings together multiple perspectives to show how restriction is both normalized and quietly questioned in private spaces.
response.tagline: A documentary on gendered silence and everyday constraint in South Asi
response.title: A Life in Pardah
